metadata.schema: per-product: sku, name, category, description, target_cohort, estimated_cogs, estimated_price, margin_target, dependencies, supplier_sources, prep_effort, launch_month, customer_feedback_signal, cross_sell_bundle, success_metric
metadata.cogs_basis: imputed at $25/hour opportunity cost for solo operator; digital products have zero marginal cost per unit after development
metadata.margin_basis: gross margin after Etsy fees (~15% combined: 6.5% transaction + 3%+$0.25 payment + $0.20/listing amortized); no per-unit COGS beyond development amortization
metadata.margin_target_all: 85%+ gross margin on all Phase 3 digital products; bundles 86-88%

products.name: Beginner Canning Quick-Start Guide
products.description: 30-35 page entry-level canning reference expanding the canning section of the Phase 1 Harvest Preservation Field Manual. Covers equipment selection (water bath vs. pressure canner), the five best first vegetables to can, headspace and processing time reference table, safety checklist, and common-failure troubleshooting. Positions as the lower-commitment gateway to the full preservation suite for buyers who find the $16 comprehensive manual intimidating.
products.dependencies: Harvest Preservation Field Manual ($16) — derives from its canning section. Buyers of the full manual are upgrade-excluded; this targets first-time buyers who search 'beginner canning guide' rather than 'harvest preservation.' Phase 1 gate: Harvest Preservation Field Manual must be live and indexed before launch.
supplier_sources: Primary: Internal content from harvest-preservation-field-manual.md (existing Phase 1 product file)
Secondary: USDA National Center for Home Food Preservation (nchfp.uga.edu) for processing time verification
products.prep_effort: 5 hours — content editing and expansion from existing Phase 1 file; 1 hour PDF generation via generate_pdfs.py; 1 hour Canva mockup; 0.5 hour listing creation. Total: 7.5 hours. No original research required.
products.customer_feedback_signal: Harvest Preservation Field Manual in Phase 1 top-3 converting products, OR Etsy messages asking 'Is there a beginner version?' or 'I've never canned before' — signals the entry-price gap below $16.
products.cross_sell_bundle: Preservation Bundle ($38) as upgrade; Preservation Season Starter Bundle (new, $22) as peer bundle with Fermentation Starter Guide and Dehydrating Guide
products.success_metric: $200/month gross by Month 4 (August 2026); 2.5%+ Etsy conversion rate within 30 days of launch

products.name: Fermentation Starter Kit Guide
products.description: 25-35 page lacto-fermentation guide derived from the Phase 1 Fermented Harvest Handbook. Covers salt ratios, jar equipment, and five first ferments (sauerkraut, kimchi, vegetable pickles, fermented hot sauce, brine-brined vegetables). Targets buyers who want vegetables-only fermentation, not the full vinegar-preservation and live-culture spectrum of the existing handbook. Entry price for the fermentation search cluster.
products.dependencies: Fermented Harvest Handbook ($13) and Grow Your Own Hot Sauce ($15) — derives from the fermentation section of the handbook. Serves buyers who found the handbook in search but want a lower-priced entry point, and buyers who bought the hot sauce guide and want to expand into fermentation.
supplier_sources: Primary: Internal content from fermented-preserved-harvest-handbook.md (existing Phase 1 product file)
Secondary: Wild Fermentation by Sandor Katz (public domain fermentation techniques for salt-ratio reference); no commercial sourcing required
products.prep_effort: 4 hours editing from existing Phase 1 content; 1 hour PDF generation; 1 hour Canva mockup; 0.5 hour listing. Total: 6.5 hours. No original research required.
products.customer_feedback_signal: Fermented Harvest Handbook in Phase 1 top-5 converting products by revenue, OR buyer search patterns showing 'fermentation for beginners' traffic — confirms entry-price gap in the fermentation sub-niche.
products.cross_sell_bundle: Preservation Bundle ($38); Preservation Season Starter Bundle ($22) with Beginner Canning Guide and Dehydrating Guide
products.success_metric: $175/month gross by Month 4; 2%+ Etsy conversion rate; cross-sell from Hot Sauce Guide buyers appearing in Etsy referral traffic

products.name: Dehydrating and Drying Field Guide
products.description: 35-45 page standalone dehydrating reference expanding the dehydrating section of the Phase 1 Harvest Preservation Field Manual. Covers dehydrator selection, oven drying, air-drying herbs, solar drying setup, time-and-temperature reference table for 40 produce items, rehydration instructions, and shelf-life data. Dehydrating is structurally underserved on Etsy compared to canning; key audience is backpackers, off-grid homesteaders, and food-storage preppers who skew toward this method.
products.dependencies: Harvest Preservation Field Manual ($16) and Meat/Fish Preservation Field Manual ($18) — derives from the dehydrating section of the former and cross-sells to the latter for buyers dehydrating meat. Can also be promoted to buyers of the Hunting/Fishing Manual ($20) who need a way to preserve wild game in the field.
supplier_sources: Primary: Internal content from harvest-preservation-field-manual.md (dehydrating section)
Secondary: USDA FoodSafety.gov dehydrating temperature guidelines (public domain); National Center for Home Food Preservation dehydrating tables
products.prep_effort: 6 hours editing and expansion from existing Phase 1 file; 1 hour PDF generation; 1 hour Canva mockup; 0.5 hour listing. Total: 8.5 hours. No original research required.
products.customer_feedback_signal: Harvest Preservation Field Manual converting at 1%+ (confirms preservation category is viable); OR Etsy search traffic data showing 'food dehydrator guide' as a traffic source to any Phase 1 listing — confirms dehydrating sub-niche has search volume.
products.cross_sell_bundle: Preservation Season Starter Bundle ($22); Master Preserver Bundle ($52, all 7 preservation products)
products.success_metric: $220/month gross by Month 4; 2%+ conversion rate; included in Preservation Season Starter Bundle purchase at least 30% of the time

products.name: Pressure Canning Meat and Poultry Guide
products.description: 40-50 page specialist guide merging the meat-handling sections of the Meat/Fish Preservation Field Manual with the pressure canning methods in the Harvest Preservation Field Manual. Covers pressure canning safety protocols for meat, processing times for chicken/beef/pork/venison/fish, broth and stock canning, and a field-dressing-to-jar workflow for hunters. Most Etsy canning guides avoid meat because of safety complexity — this is the gap. The audience (hunters, livestock raisers) is high-purchase-intent with few alternatives.
products.dependencies: Hunting/Fishing Manual ($20), Livestock Manual ($18), and Meat/Fish Preservation Field Manual ($18) — serves buyers of any of these three Phase 1 products who want to complete the harvest-to-jar workflow. Cross-promotes naturally via Homesteader's Complete Bundle ($50).
supplier_sources: Primary: Internal content from meat-fish-preservation-field-manual.md and harvest-preservation-field-manual.md
Secondary: USDA Complete Guide to Home Canning (Bulletin 539-B, public domain) for meat processing time tables — authoritative source required for safety claims
products.prep_effort: 7 hours — merging and editing content from two existing Phase 1 files; safety protocol verification against USDA source (1 hour); PDF generation (1 hour); Canva mockup (1 hour); listing (0.5 hour). Total: 10.5 hours.
products.customer_feedback_signal: Hunting/Fishing Manual or Meat/Fish Preservation Field Manual in Phase 1 top-5 by revenue — confirms the hunter/livestock audience is purchasing and validates the specialist sub-niche extension. Alternatively, Etsy messages asking 'Does this cover canning venison?' or 'Can I pressure can chicken?'
products.cross_sell_bundle: Homesteader's Complete Bundle ($50); Master Preserver Bundle ($52)
products.success_metric: $280/month gross by Month 5; 2%+ Etsy conversion rate; purchased together with Hunting/Fishing Manual in at least 15% of transactions involving that product

products.name: Wild Edibles Quick Reference — 18 Common North American Wild Edibles
products.description: 22-25 page visual identification reference using the 18 wild-edibles habit photos completed in Session 560 (all CC BY-SA licensed from Wikimedia Commons). One page per species: habit photo, common name, species name, 3 key identification features, lookalike warning where applicable, harvest season, edible parts, and preparation method. Not a foraging safety manual — that is served by the Native Plants Regional Guide. This is a compact visual companion reference for the 18 most useful North American wild edibles. Includes a photo attribution page as required by CC BY-SA license terms.
products.dependencies: Native Plants Regional Guide ($18) — the natural visual companion to the text-heavy regional guide. Buyers of the regional guide get a detailed reference; buyers of this Quick Reference are prompted to upgrade to the full regional guide for their area. Also cross-sells to Regional Self-Sufficiency Bundle ($28). Hard dependency: 18 wild-edibles habit photos must be downloaded and archived before layout begins (completed Session 560).
supplier_sources: Primary: projects/seedwarden/assets/wild-edibles/*.jpg — 18 CC BY-SA species photos from Wikimedia Commons, all logged in WORKLOG.md Session 560
Secondary: iNaturalist (inaturalist.org) for species identification verification before publication; USDA PLANTS database for common name confirmation
products.prep_effort: 10 hours layout (design-intensive — one-page-per-species format in Canva); 1 hour attribution page creation; 1 hour PDF generation via generate_pdfs.py; 1 hour listing. Total: 13 hours. Photos already sourced; no additional image work required.
products.customer_feedback_signal: Native Plants Regional Guide in Phase 1 top-3 converting products — confirms forager cohort is purchasing and validates visual identification product investment. Alternatively, high Pinterest saves on any foraging-related content pin signals visual-format demand.
products.cross_sell_bundle: Regional Self-Sufficiency Bundle ($28, existing Phase 1 bundle); Regional Forager Bundle (new Phase 3 bundle: Wild Edibles Quick Reference + Native Plants Regional Guide, $26)
products.success_metric: $200/month gross by Month 4; purchased together with Native Plants Regional Guide in at least 20% of Native Plants transactions; 15%+ repurchase rate from forager cohort within 90 days

products.name: Wild Edibles Habitat Photo Pack — 18 Species Digital Download
products.description: ZIP package of all 18 wild-edibles habit photos from the Seedwarden assets library, delivered as high-resolution JPEG files. Packaged with ATTRIBUTION.txt (source URL and CC BY-SA license per image) and README.txt explaining reuse terms. Audience: bloggers, homeschool curriculum creators, foraging instructors, nature educators, and content creators who need licensed botanical photography. Not a royalty-free commercial pack — buyers may use with attribution under CC BY-SA. Must be stated clearly in listing description to set correct expectations.
products.dependencies: Requires wild-edibles habit photos to be downloaded and archived (completed Session 560). No Phase 1 product dependency — this product can launch independently of Phase 1 conversion data. Secondary dependency: Wild Edibles Quick Reference (SW-P3-05) should launch first to establish the forager audience before the photo pack.
supplier_sources: Primary: projects/seedwarden/assets/wild-edibles/*.jpg — all 18 images from Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0 or 4.0; source URLs logged in WORKLOG.md
Secondary: Wikimedia Commons (commons.wikimedia.org) for license text and attribution formatting requirements
products.prep_effort: 3 hours — ZIP packaging, ATTRIBUTION.txt creation (one line per image: filename, species, photographer, source URL, license), README.txt writing, test download verification. No content creation required.
products.customer_feedback_signal: Forager cohort identified as 20%+ of Phase 1 buyers (per cohort survey responses); OR Etsy messages asking about photo rights or whether images can be reprinted — signals the educator/creator sub-audience is present.
products.cross_sell_bundle: Regional Forager Bundle ($26); can be added as optional add-on to any foraging bundle listing via description cross-link
products.success_metric: $120/month gross by Month 5; 2+ Etsy reviews from educator or homeschool buyers confirming use case; conversion rate above 1.5%

products.name: Native Plants Identification Flashcard Set — 40 Plants, Printable
products.description: 40-50 printable plant identification cards in 3x5 inch format, derived from the Native Plants Regional Guide. Each card: botanical illustration or photo, common name, species name, 3 identification features, habitat notes, edibility note, and one lookalike warning. Delivered as PDF with one-card-per-page layout for index card printing and a 4-up layout for letter paper. Audience: teachers, homeschoolers, nature journalers, foraging course instructors, and trail guides who need a tactile, printable tool rather than a full-length PDF guide.
products.dependencies: Native Plants Regional Guide ($18) — all card content derives from the guide's per-species entries. Buyers of the guide get the full text reference; buyers of the flashcards get the condensed tactile format. Conditional on forager cohort signal: if forager cohort is below 15% of Phase 1 buyers by Month 2, deprioritize to Month 5.
supplier_sources: Primary: Internal content from native-plants-regional-guide.md (existing Phase 1 product file)
Secondary: Wikimedia Commons botanical photos (commons.wikimedia.org) for card illustrations where available; iNaturalist for species verification
products.prep_effort: 14 hours card layout in Canva (design-intensive — individual card design for 40+ species, two layout variants); 1 hour PDF generation and print-test verification; 1 hour listing. Total: 16 hours.
products.customer_feedback_signal: Forager cohort making up 20%+ of buyers, OR homeschool/educator buyers identified in post-purchase survey responses — confirms flashcard format has a real audience. If forager cohort is below 15% of buyers by Month 2, deprioritize to Month 5.
products.cross_sell_bundle: Regional Self-Sufficiency Bundle ($28); Regional Forager Bundle ($26)
products.success_metric: $175/month gross by Month 5; identified as a top-3 product in 'nature study' or 'homeschool' search terms via Etsy traffic sources

products.name: Seed Library Setup and Organization System — Complete with Printable Templates
products.description: 40-50 page seed library organization system including 10 printable templates: seed envelope labels (3 sizes), seed catalog record card, germination tracking log, annual planting-out calendar, variety description card, trade and swap sheet, viability chart by species, storage conditions reference, seed library binder cover, and section divider tabs. Addresses the post-seed-saving organization problem that the Phase 1 Seed Saving Field Manual does not cover. These are different products serving different stages of the seed-saving practice.
products.dependencies: Seed Saving Field Manual ($14) and Seed Swap Hosting Kit ($10) — natural next-step purchase for buyers who have started saving seeds (Field Manual) or hosting swaps (Swap Kit) and now need an organizational system. Also cross-sells to Food Sovereignty Starter Guide ($8) buyers who are building their first seed collection. No seasonal deadline — can launch any time September–November without significant conversion impact.
supplier_sources: Primary: Internal content from seed-saving-field-manual.md for viability chart and storage conditions data
Secondary: Seed Savers Exchange (seedsavers.org) for variety viability benchmarks; USDA AMS Seed Program for germination standard reference
products.prep_effort: 10 hours — guide writing (5 hours) + 10-template design in Canva (4 hours) + PDF generation and print-test verification (1 hour); 0.5 hour listing. Total: 10.5 hours.
products.customer_feedback_signal: Seed Saving Field Manual in Phase 1 top-5 by revenue, OR Etsy messages asking 'How do I organize my seeds?' or 'Does the guide include templates?' — direct signals that the organizational gap exists and buyers have found it.
products.cross_sell_bundle: Food Sovereignty Bundle ($30, existing Phase 1 bundle) — the Seed Library System is the natural complement to the four existing sovereignty guides
products.success_metric: $280/month gross by Month 6; Seed Saving Field Manual buyers purchasing this product within 30 days at a rate of 10%+ (tracked via UTM-tagged email Day 7 cross-sell)

products.name: Medicinal Herb Growing and Harvest Guide — 20 Herbs from Seed to Storage
products.description: 50-60 page cultivation and preservation guide for 20 commonly grown medicinal herbs: chamomile, lavender, echinacea, elderberry, lemon balm, holy basil, calendula, yarrow, mullein, valerian, rosemary, peppermint, St. John's wort, feverfew, comfrey, and 5 regionally determined species. Per-herb entries cover growing requirements, container vs. in-ground, harvest timing and technique, drying and processing methods, and preparation notes (teas, tinctures, poultices — description only). No medical claims. Standard educational disclaimer. Herb photos sourced from Wikimedia Commons (20 images, CC-licensed, attribution page required).
products.dependencies: Native Plants Regional Guide ($18) — the most adjacent Phase 1 product by topic. Also cross-sells from Harvest Preservation Field Manual ($16) for buyers who are preserving herb harvests. Conditional dependency: if Phase 1 data shows no botanical-adjacent demand (forager cohort below 20% of buyers AND Native Plants Regional Guide below 1.5% conversion by July 1), deprioritize to Phase 4.
supplier_sources: Primary: USDA PLANTS Database (plants.usda.gov) for species botanical verification and common name accuracy
Secondary: iNaturalist (inaturalist.org) for regional distribution verification; Wikimedia Commons for 20 herb photos (CC-licensed; attribution page required)
products.prep_effort: 12 hours new research and writing (highest content effort in Phase 3); 2 hours Wikimedia Commons image sourcing (20 herb photos); 1 hour attribution page; 1 hour PDF generation; 1 hour Canva mockup; 0.5 hour listing. Total: 17.5 hours.
products.customer_feedback_signal: Forager cohort at 20%+ of Phase 1 buyers AND Native Plants Regional Guide converting at 1.5%+ — dual signal confirming the botanical-content audience is real and paying. If either signal is absent by July 1, move this product to Phase 4.
products.cross_sell_bundle: Expanded Homesteader Gift Set ($62, Phase 3 anchor gift bundle); Regional Self-Sufficiency Bundle ($28) as future Phase 4 bundle update candidate
products.success_metric: $250/month gross by Month 6; 1.5%+ Etsy conversion rate; appears in Etsy search results for 'medicinal herb guide' within 60 days of launch (tracked via eRank or Marmalead)

products.name: Homestead Skills Assessment and Learning Roadmap
products.description: 30-40 page self-assessment and structured learning pathway for people building homesteading or food-sovereignty skills. Covers: current-skills inventory checklist (100 skills across 8 categories: gardening, seed saving, preservation, foraging, livestock, wild harvest, community, and preparedness), beginner/intermediate/advanced skill tiers, recommended learning sequence for each skill set, 12-month first-year homesteader learning calendar, and resource recommendations that explicitly cross-reference specific Seedwarden products at each skill tier. Simultaneously a standalone value product and the most effective catalog navigation tool in the Phase 3 lineup.
products.dependencies: Food Sovereignty Starter Guide ($8) and Companion Planting Chart ($5) as primary entry-point products this extends. Soft dependency: benefits significantly from having multiple Phase 3 products live so cross-references within the guide point to real listings. Sequence after at least 8 Phase 3 products are live.
supplier_sources: Primary: All existing Phase 1 product files — content synthesized across all 21 guides for the skills taxonomy and product cross-reference map
Secondary: Homestead.org and iNaturalist skill learning pathways for skills taxonomy benchmarking; no commercial sourcing required
products.prep_effort: 8 hours — skills taxonomy construction and writing (5 hours) + checklist and calendar layouts in Canva (2 hours) + PDF generation (0.5 hour) + listing (0.5 hour). Total: 8 hours. No original research required.
products.customer_feedback_signal: Homesteader cohort at 30%+ of Phase 1 buyers (expected baseline) AND average order value below $20 — signals buyers are purchasing individual products without a clear cross-sell path, which is exactly the problem this product solves. Also triggered by Etsy search traffic from 'homesteading for beginners' keywords reaching any Phase 1 listing.
products.cross_sell_bundle: Universal cross-sell to all existing bundles — the skills roadmap sections each point to a different bundle. Primary bundle pathway: Food Sovereignty Bundle ($30) for sovereignty-track buyers; Homesteader's Complete Bundle ($50) for skills-comprehensive buyers
products.success_metric: Average order value increases by $5+ in the 30 days after launch (indicating it is triggering bundle upgrades); $200/month gross by Month 7; purchased as a second product by 15%+ of new customers within 30 days of their first purchase

products.name: First-Year Food Preservation Planner — Printable Seasonal Planning Journal
products.description: 55-60 page printable planner organized around the preservation calendar from July through February. Monthly sections include: harvest readiness tracker, preservation goal-setting, supplies checklist for each method (canning, fermenting, dehydrating, freezing), batch production log (what was preserved, date, quantity, storage location), gift and sharing log, and next-year notes. Designed to be printed and bound — the tactile, journal-like format creates higher gift potential than a reference manual. Serves both committed homesteaders planning a full preservation season and gift buyers seeking a product that feels substantial.
products.dependencies: Harvest Preservation Field Manual ($16) and Fermented Harvest Handbook ($13) — the planner provides the planning infrastructure supplementing those manuals. Natural post-purchase follow-up: Day 7 email to preservation manual buyers introduces this as the seasonal planning companion. October launch timed for holiday gift window.
supplier_sources: Primary: Original planner design — no Phase 1 content derives directly into this product; layout and structure requires original creation
Secondary: Canva (canva.com) for printable planner layout — section headers, calendar grids, log tables, cover design; no external content sourcing required
products.prep_effort: 10 hours original planner design in Canva (layout-intensive: monthly spreads, tracker tables, checklist grids); 1 hour PDF generation and print-test at home; 0.5 hour listing. Total: 11.5 hours.
products.customer_feedback_signal: Gift buyer cohort at 15%+ of Phase 1 buyers AND preservation products generating 25%+ of Phase 1 revenue — confirms both the gift audience and the preservation category are delivering enough volume to justify a gift-positioned preservation product.
products.cross_sell_bundle: Preservation Bundle ($38, existing Phase 1 bundle); Master Preserver Bundle ($52, new Phase 3 bundle)
products.success_metric: $250/month gross by Month 7 (October + November holiday lift); gift buyer cohort purchasing this product at 20%+ of gift bundle purchases during October-December window

products.name: The Homesteader's Complete Reference Set — Expanded Gift Bundle
products.description: Seven-guide expanded gift bundle combining the four Phase 1 Homesteader's Complete Bundle products (Small-Scale Livestock Field Manual, Hunting/Fishing Manual, Harvest Preservation Field Manual, Meat/Fish Preservation Field Manual) with three Phase 3 additions (Wild Edibles Quick Reference, Medicinal Herb Guide, Homestead Skills Assessment and Learning Roadmap). Individual total $106; bundle price $62 (save $44, 41% off). The $44 savings figure and the 7-guide scope are the primary conversion drivers for the holiday gift buyer. Positioned as 'the most complete homesteading reference library available on Etsy.' Launches October 15 to capture early gift browsing.
products.dependencies: Hard dependency: all three Phase 3 constituent products must be live before this bundle listing goes live — Wild Edibles Quick Reference (SW-P3-05, M3), Medicinal Herb Guide (SW-P3-09, M5), Homestead Skills Assessment (SW-P3-10, M6). Sequence: launch October 15 after SW-P3-10 confirms live on October 1. Phase 1 dependency: Homesteader's Complete Bundle ($50) must be in Phase 1 top-5 revenue generators.
supplier_sources: Primary: All constituent PDFs already developed across Phase 1 and Phase 3 — no new content creation required
Secondary: Canva for multi-product mockup showing all 7 guide covers; Etsy automatic digital download for ZIP delivery
products.prep_effort: 2 hours — listing creation only (title, description, tags, price, ZIP bundle packaging); Canva multi-product mockup (1 hour); Etsy listing setup (1 hour). Zero content development required.
products.customer_feedback_signal: Gift buyer cohort at 15%+ of Phase 1 buyers, AND Homesteader's Complete Bundle ($50) appearing in top-5 Phase 1 revenue generators — confirms the gift-buyer appetite for premium bundles. Also triggered by any significant October spike in bundle-listing traffic (early holiday browsing signal).
products.cross_sell_bundle: This IS the top-tier bundle. Cross-sells from: Homesteader's Complete Bundle ($50) as an upgrade; any individual Phase 1 product for buyers who want to give a comprehensive gift rather than a single guide
products.success_metric: $500/month gross during October-December window (3-month gift season target); average of 1 unit sold per day during the Black Friday week (November 27-30); appears in Etsy search results for 'homesteading gift' within 45 days of launch

phase3_regional_listings.note: 14 regional variant listings (7 Native Plants, 7 Survival Garden) are not included in the 12-product spec above because they use existing Phase 1 PDFs with no new content. They are the highest-ROI Phase 3 action per development hour.

bundle_summary.name: Preservation Season Starter Bundle
contents: SW-P3-03 Dehydrating and Drying Field Guide

bundle_summary.name: Master Preserver Bundle — Complete Home Preservation Library
contents: SW-P3-03 Dehydrating and Drying Field Guide
bundle_summary.success_metric: $400/month gross during August-October; primary conversion driver is the $38 savings figure

bundle_summary.name: Regional Forager Bundle
bundle_summary.pricing_note: Shallow discount — value is convenience, not savings. Deepen to $24 (14% off) if conversion below 1% at 30 days.

products.rationale: 7,717-line, 9-region, 80-plant guide priced at the bottom of the premium segment. Regional listings at $12 create favorable contrast: the complete guide at $22 is clearly the better value vs. $12 per region.
products.revert_trigger: Conversion rate drops more than 25% vs. 30-day July baseline

products.rationale: Individual region listings at $5.99 imply $41.93 total for all 7 regions, making $24 for the complete bundle read as a 43% discount.
products.revert_trigger: Conversion rate drops more than 25% vs. 30-day July baseline

products.rationale: Most specialized, lowest-competition product in the Phase 1 catalog. Specialist buyers have higher price tolerance. No adjacent Phase 1 product would be cannibalized by this increase.
products.revert_trigger: Conversion rate drops more than 25% vs. 30-day July baseline

revenue_targets.note: Gross revenue before Etsy fees (~15% combined). Phase 1 baseline established May-June 2026.

monthly.label: M6 gift season + full catalog live

phase3_repeat_purchase_targets.month_3: 15% repeat rate (customers purchasing more than once within 90 days)

aov_targets.driver: Bundle adoption — if AOV is flat despite bundle availability, add 'frequently bought together' language to individual product descriptions
